methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Orchid is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Noor Siddiqui that has raised $16.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 private companies at Series B stage and 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), selected for sector alignment and stage proximity.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $33M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies produces $805M. This peer set ranges from $100M (floor) to $3.3B (ceiling), with a median of $805M derived from companies including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Orchid's revenue is not disclosed, precluding use of the sector P/S median of 7.8x across the three public tickers.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $28M to $926M, with a median of $419M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding whether Orchid's progress and capital efficiency track more closely to the stage-progression baseline or to later-stage peer valuations. The peer-median method carries greater weight given the specificity of the comp set, though revenue trajectory and clinical/commercial milestones remain critical to narrowing the range.
